task_description: Consensus-plan the argus-agent Thread-first lock-free redesign using .omx/specs/deep-interview-argus-agent-thread-core-lock-free.md as source of truth.

review_summary.architect_round_1: ITERATE on seam split, SessionId contradiction, ownership inversion, session migration scope
review_summary.critic_round_1: REJECT on migration order, SessionId contradiction, shallow alternatives, weak verification

final_decision: Stage the extraction: test first, invert root ownership, split helper seams and session-owned ThreadSessionIndex, introduce argus-thread-pool for pure pool-core, preserve session_id compatibility via adapter outside pool-core.
residual_risk: none blocking; protocol cleanup intentionally deferred

key_feedback: plan stale vs current repo state
retain tradeoff note about eventual consistency vs direct-read convenience
keep mandatory verifier/code-reviewer pass because grep residue checks are heuristic

recommended_execution.ralph: preferred if one owner should execute sequentially through the defined phase gates
recommended_execution.team: appropriate after the owner/handle contract is fixed enough for disjoint pool/session/job lanes
